description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a batch deployment for three IoT devices.  
All raw configuration files are already present on the system as classic INI files:

• /home/user/iot_configs/device_alpha.ini  
• /home/user/iot_configs/device_beta.ini  
• /home/user/iot_configs/device_gamma.ini  

Each INI file uses the exact same structure and required keys shown below.  
(The agent must treat any key-name lookup as case-insensitive.)

-------------------------------------------------
[Network]
ip = <IPv4 address>
location = <free-text location string>

[Sensors]
enabled = <comma-separated sensor list>

[System]
os = <operating-system string>
last_maintained = <YYYY-MM-DD>
-------------------------------------------------

Task goals
==========

1. Create the directory /home/user/deployment if it does not already exist.

2. Produce a single machine-readable CSV summarising the contents of EVERY
   *.ini file found in /home/user/iot_configs.  
   The file must be named:
       /home/user/deployment/device_report.csv

   CSV formatting rules (strictly enforced by automated tests):

   a. First row is the header line, written exactly as:

      DeviceID,IP,Location,EnabledSensors,OS,LastMaintained

   b. One subsequent row per device (row order does NOT matter).  
      • DeviceID is the original base filename without extension
        (e.g. “device_alpha” for “device_alpha.ini”).  
      • EnabledSensors must be the sensor list from the INI but converted to
        “pipe” separators instead of commas (e.g. “temp|humidity|pressure”).  
      • NO surrounding quotes are allowed anywhere in the file.  
      • All fields are separated by a single literal comma.  
      • The file must terminate with a single trailing newline.

3. Generate a plain-text validation log verifying that the mandatory keys
   (ip, location, enabled, os, last_maintained) exist for every device:

       /home/user/deployment/validation.log

   Log formatting rules (strictly enforced):

   • Exactly one line per device in the format:

        [<DeviceID>] Validation: <STATUS>

   • <STATUS> must be “OK” if every required key exists and is non-empty;   
     otherwise it must be “MISSING:<comma-separated list of absent keys>”.  
   • Lines may appear in any order, but there must be no extra whitespace
     before or after each line.  
   • The file must terminate with a single trailing newline.

4. When finished, you should be able to run the two commands below and see
   output similar to (ordering may differ):

   cat /home/user/deployment/device_report.csv
   cat /home/user/deployment/validation.log

   The CSV should list all three devices and the log should indicate “OK” for the
   current dataset.  Automated grading will compare your output files bit-for-bit
   against the required format above, so pay close attention to commas, pipes,
   capitalisation and newlines.

You do NOT need sudo privileges.  Feel free to use any Bash, Awk, Python or
other CLI tools available in a typical Debian-based container to complete the
task.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the agent starts):

/home/user/iot_configs/device_alpha.ini
---------------------------------------
[Network]
ip = 192.168.10.11
location = RoofTop

[Sensors]
enabled = temp,humidity,pressure

[System]
os = TinyLinux
last_maintained = 2023-05-10


/home/user/iot_configs/device_beta.ini
--------------------------------------
[Network]
ip = 192.168.10.12
location = Warehouse55

[Sensors]
enabled = temp,vibration

[System]
os = TinyLinux
last_maintained = 2023-04-01


/home/user/iot_configs/device_gamma.ini
---------------------------------------
[Network]
ip = 192.168.10.13
location = Dock

[Sensors]
enabled = temp,humidity,air_quality,co2

[System]
os = TinyLinux
last_maintained = 2022-12-15


Expected artefacts AFTER a correct run by the agent:

1. Directory:
   /home/user/deployment          (exists, standard user permissions)

2. File:
   /home/user/deployment/device_report.csv
   Exact content (final newline shown as ↵):

DeviceID,IP,Location,EnabledSensors,OS,LastMaintained↵
device_alpha,192.168.10.11,RoofTop,temp|humidity|pressure,TinyLinux,2023-05-10↵
device_beta,192.168.10.12,Warehouse55,temp|vibration,TinyLinux,2023-04-01↵
device_gamma,192.168.10.13,Dock,temp|humidity|air_quality|co2,TinyLinux,2022-12-15↵


3. File:
   /home/user/deployment/validation.log
   Exact content (final newline shown as ↵):

[device_alpha] Validation: OK↵
[device_beta] Validation: OK↵
[device_gamma] Validation: OK↵
name: task_000000_1f347e2d
